note: Created KeyPinningAndZeroingTests.cs with 15 tests verifying: (1) KeyMarshaller uses fixed statements for pinning, (2) no string copies of key material, (3) pinned addresses are stable under GC pressure, (4) key buffers can be zeroed with Array.Clear and CryptographicOperations.ZeroMemory, (5) key-holding types store keys as byte[] (pinnable/zeroable), (6) full lifecycle tests: create, pin, zero.
